title: Vibrant Crisp Japanese Oxtail Skewers
description: Mustardy, crisp, comforting yakitori-style skewers with tender oxtail glazed in a tangy mustard-ginger sauce and finished with fresh scallion.
instructions: Preheat a grill or skillet over medium-high heat and lightly oil the grates or pan.
In a bowl, whisk together Japanese mustard, minced ginger, soy sauce, mirin, sugar, water, and black pepper until the sugar dissolves.
Pat the oxtail pieces dry with paper towels and season them lightly with salt; thread each piece onto bamboo skewers, spacing them evenly.
Brush the oxtail skewers generously with the mustard glaze, reserving a small amount for later.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill and sear for 3 minutes on each side, turning carefully to avoid flare‑ups.
Reduce the heat to medium, brush with the remaining glaze, and continue cooking, turning every 2 minutes, until the oxtail is browned and a glaze coats every surface, about 20-25 minutes total.
Remove the skewers and let them rest for 5 minutes to allow juices to redistribute.
While resting, drizzle any leftover glaze over the skewers for an extra shine.
Garnish the finished skewers with chopped scallions and a light dusting of sesame seeds if desired.
Serve hot with steamed rice or a side of miso soup for a comforting meal.
